2606.11889.title: Task-Aligned Stability Analysis of Vision-Language Models for Autonomous Driving Hazard Detection
2606.11889.paper_summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) are increasingly used for scene understanding in autonomous driving, but robustness analysis often relies on task-agnostic embedding stability alone. We study whether corruption-induced embedding drift predicts changes in a task-aligned hazard score derived from CLIP image-text similarities. Using controlled corruptions on BDD100K road scenes, we compare embedding drift against margin drift, defined as the change in hazard score under perturbation. The relationship is highly corruption-dependent: some families exhibit strong coupling between representation drift and decision drift, while others induce hazardous decision instability despite relatively modest embedding change. Furthermore, corruption families differ in failure direction: most suppress hazard detections via false negatives, while occlusion instead triggers false alarms, suggesting that benchmark design should account for asymmetric failure modes, not just overall instability rates. These results suggest that robustness benchmarks should include task-aligned stability measures in addition to embedding-level perturbation statistics.
2606.11889.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在自動駕駛中的場景理解上越來越被使用，但穩健性分析通常僅依賴於與任務無關的嵌入穩定性。我們研究由腐敗引起的嵌入漂移是否能預測來自 CLIP 圖像-文本相似性的任務對齊危險分數的變化。使用對 BDD100K 道路場景的控制腐敗，我們將嵌入漂移與邊際漂移進行比較，後者定義為在擾動下危險分數的變化。這種關係高度依賴於腐敗：一些家族在表示漂移和決策漂移之間表現出強耦合，而另一些則在相對適度的嵌入變化下引發危險的決策不穩定。此外，腐敗家族在失敗方向上有所不同：大多數通過假陰性來抑制危險檢測，而遮擋則觸發假警報，這表明基準設計應考慮不對稱的失敗模式，而不僅僅是整體不穩定性率。這些結果表明，穩健性基準應包括與任務對齊的穩定性指標，除了嵌入級別的擾動統計外。
2606.11889.author: Everett Richards et.al.
